sevenDayForecast.headline: Severe Tropical Cyclone Errol has intensified to a category 3 cyclone.
points: Tropical Cyclone Information Bulletins and Forecast Track Maps are being issued for Severe Tropical Cyclone Errol and should be referred to for more detailed information.
Severe Tropical Cyclone Errol is expected to intensify a category 4 tonight, before beginning to weaken from Thursday afternoon.
Errol is currently moving west, but is expected to turn to the southeast from tonight and begin moving towards the west Kimberley coast on Thursday.

trackForecast.headline: Severe Tropical Cyclone Errol has intensified to a category 3 cyclone. It is expected to recurve towards the west Kimberley coast on Thursday.
trackForecast.discussion:
Severe Tropical Cyclone Errol (29U) has intensified to a category 3 cyclone. Conditions are favourable for further intensification. A peak intensity of a category 4 is a possibility within the next 24 hours.

Severe Tropical Cyclone Errol (29U) is moving westwards but is expected to recurve towards the southeast on Thursday as an upper trough captures it and moves it to the southeast.

Increasing wind shear and dry air associated with the upper trough is likely to affect its intensity. Errol is forecast to drop to a category 1 or below tropical cyclone intensity overnight on Friday or Saturday morning, as it nears the west Kimberley coast.

The extension of the 120 hour confidence cone towards offshore Pilbara waters is representing scenarios of a remnant tropical low moving back towards the west over the weekend. A tropical cyclone is very unlikely to exist in this area.
trackForecast.discussionUpperBound: The upper bound scenario is based on a period of sustained rapid intensification over the next 24 hours. As Errol is a small system, it is prone to rapid fluctuations in intensity. The upper bound from later Thursday reflects a delay in the weakening associated with the negative impacts of the upper trough, allowing the system to remain stronger than forecast as it moves nearer the Kimberley coast.
